source.title: Central cancellation of self-produced tickle sensation

confidence_rationale: Directly demonstrates the central mechanism that makes MECH-071 neurobiologically plausible: cerebellar forward models predict the sensory consequences of self-generated movement and attenuate the somatosensory cortical response. External stimulation produces greater somatosensory cortex activation than identical self-produced stimulation, establishing that the brain differentially encodes self-generated versus externally-generated sensory events. The mapping to E2 as efference-copy forward model and the self/environment asymmetry in harm prediction is direct. Confidence capped at 0.80 because the domain is tactile sensation (not harm evaluation) and the stimuli are benign.
failure_signatures: If E2 cannot distinguish self-generated from environment-generated harm events at the latent level, this cerebellar forward-model architecture would predict identical somatosensory responses -- the opposite of what the paper shows.

mapping.source_claim_statement: The cerebellum generates predictions of the sensory consequences of self-produced movements; these predictions cancel (attenuate) the somatosensory cortical response, so self-produced tactile stimuli produce less somatosensory cortex activation than externally produced identical stimuli.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-071 posits that E2 harm prediction is better calibrated for agent-caused transitions than environment-caused ones. This maps directly: E2 functions as the efference-copy forward model -- it receives motor commands (z_self, SD-005) and predicts resulting sensory-world states including harm consequences. When the agent acts, E2 has advance prediction of the outcome; when the environment acts on the agent, no efference copy exists and the harm signal arrives unpredicted. The cerebellar-cortical attenuation gradient is the precise neural mechanism that would make agent-caused harm MORE predictable (lower surprise, better calibrated) while environment-caused harm generates larger, less-attenuated prediction errors.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The paper uses benign tactile tickle stimuli, not harm events. The claim that the same forward-model architecture extends to harm evaluation requires an additional step -- that E2/E3 harm evaluation circuits receive analogous efference-copy signals. This is architecturally supported by MECH-070 (E2 as conceptual sensorium motor model) but is not directly demonstrated in this paper.
mapping.source_context: Human fMRI; healthy young adults; self-produced vs externally-produced tactile stimulation of the palm; measures somatosensory cortex and cerebellar BOLD responses.

confidence_components.notes: High source quality -- Nature Neuroscience, widely replicated, foundational. Mapping fidelity strong for the self/other asymmetry mechanism; transfer risk is the extension from benign touch to harm evaluation, and from biological motor systems to artificial latent architectures.
